content:
## English

**Yang Numbers Have Ended, and When Ended They Rise Again** is a concept from *Zhouyi Cantongqi* (Chapter 18), expressing the cyclical pattern of yin and yang. When yang reaches its limit, it declines; when it has fully declined, yang rises again. This perpetual alternation drives the cosmic mechanism (*璇璣*), the circulation of the six lines (*周流六爻*), and the convergence of new moon and full moon (*晦朔合符*). Wei Boyang viewed this principle as the very essence of the *Yijing*'s teaching: "Thus there is no fixed position, yet it is the ancestor of the *Yi* (*故無常位，為易宗祖*)." Alchemical practitioners follow this pattern of rising and falling to achieve the golden elixir.

## 中文

**陽數已訖，訖則復起**，出《[[周易參同契]]·第十八章》。陽數極則終，終則復起，迭運不息。此理貫於[[璇璣]]之運、[[周流六爻]]之道、[[晦朔合符]]之機。魏伯陽以此為《易》之宗祖，所謂[[故無常位]]，[[為易宗祖]]也。修煉者法此迭起迭伏之理，乃成金丹之功。

## 日本語

**陽數已訖、訖れば則ち復た起る**とは、『[[周易参同契]]』第十八章に見える語である。陽數が極まれば終わり、終わりせば再び起る。この迭運不息の理は、[[璇璣]]の運行・[[周流六爻]]・[[晦朔合符]]に通じる。魏伯陽はこれを以て易の宗祖とした。修煉者もまたこの迭起迭伏の道理に従い、金丹の功を成就する。
summary: A principle from Zhouyi Cantongqi describing the cyclical alternation of yin and yang in the cosmos and alchemy.
title: Yang Numbers Have Ended, and When Ended They Rise Again / 陽數已訖，訖則復起
